title: ruta angustifolia subsp. bracteosa
intro: Ruta angustifolia subsp. bracteosa, also known as a subspecies of Rue, is a perennial plant in the Rutaceae family, characterized by its narrow leaves and small yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. Ruta angustifolia subsp. bracteosa has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to soothe digestive problems and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health concerns.
